Description: The building blocks of large-scale distributed systems and performance optimization.

Principles.Title: Scalability
Principles.Effect: The ability of a system to handle increased load by adding resources without performance degradation.
BestPractices: Vertical Scaling: Adding more power (CPU, RAM) to an existing server.
Horizontal Scaling: Adding more servers to the service pool.
Stateless Architecture: Decoupling state from application servers.
Database Sharding: Partitioning data across multiple database instances.

Principles.Title: Availability
Principles.Effect: Ensuring the system remains operational and accessible even during component failures.
BestPractices: Redundancy: Eliminating single points of failure (SPOF).
Automatic Failover: Switching to standby systems during outages.
SLA Management: Targeting 99.999% ('five nines') availability.
Multi-AZ / Multi-Region: Deploying across isolated geographic zones.

Principles.Title: Reliability
Principles.Effect: The probability that a system will perform its intended function without failure for a specified period.
BestPractices: Chaos Engineering: Proactively testing system resilience.
Retries with Backoff: Implementing exponential backoff logic.
Circuit Breakers: Preventing cascading failures in dependencies.
Idempotency: Ensuring operations can be re-run safely.

Principles.Title: Latency vs Throughput
Principles.Effect: Balancing the speed of a single request against the total volume of requests processed.
BestPractices: Connection Pooling: Reusing database and network connections.
Batching: Combining multiple operations into a single request.
Asynchronous I/O: Handling requests without blocking threads.
Network Optimization: Using HTTP/3 and compression.

Description: Structural paradigms for organizing components and managing communication flow.

Principles.Title: Microservices
Principles.Effect: Decomposing an application into a collection of small, independently deployable services.
BestPractices: Single Responsibility: Each service owns one business capability.
Database per Service: Avoiding shared data sources between services.
API Gateway: Centralizing authentication, routing, and rate limiting.
Service Discovery: Automating location tracking of dynamic services.

Principles.Title: Serverless
Principles.Effect: An event-driven execution model where the provider manages server allocation and scaling.
BestPractices: Cold Start Optimization: Minimizing package size and using 'provisioned concurrency'.
Stateless Functions: Ensuring functions don't rely on local persistent state.
Fine-grained Scaling: Scaling based on specific event triggers.
Cost Awareness: Designing for short execution durations.

Principles.Title: Event-Driven (EDA)
Principles.Effect: A system architecture where events trigger and communicate between decoupled services.
BestPractices: Pub/Sub Decoupling: Ensuring publishers don't know about subscribers.
Event Sourcing: Storing state changes as an immutable sequence of events.
CQRS: Separating read and write operations for performance.
Dead Letter Queues: Handling failed message processing gracefully.

Principles.Title: Sidecar Pattern
Principles.Effect: Deploying components of an application into a separate process or container to provide isolation.
BestPractices: Abbreviated Communication: Offloading logging and monitoring to sidecars.
Service Mesh: Managing mTLS and traffic rules outside the application.
Platform Independence: Running sidecars in any language.
Resource Separation: Limiting sidecar CPU/RAM usage.

Description: Advanced strategies for persistence, consistency, and large-scale data processing.

Principles.Title: SQL vs NoSQL
Principles.Effect: Selecting a database model based on data structure, consistency, and scaling needs.
BestPractices: ACID Compliance: Choosing SQL for transactional integrity.
BASE Consistency: Choosing NoSQL for loose coupling and scaling.
Schema Evolution: Using NoSQL for rapidly changing data formats.
Polyglot Persistence: Using different databases for different needs.

Principles.Title: Caching Strategies
Principles.Effect: Improving performance by storing frequently accessed data in a fast-access layer.
BestPractices: Cache Aside: Lazy loading data into cache upon request.
Write-Through: Synchronously updating cache and DB.
TTL Management: Implementing time-to-live for data freshness.
Eviction Policies: Using LRU (Least Recently Used) algorithms.

Principles.Title: Database Sharding
Principles.Effect: Horizontal partitioning of data to distribute the load across multiple database nodes.
BestPractices: Shard Key Selection: Choosing keys that prevent 'hot shards'.
Resharding Strategy: Planning for data migration as volume grows.
Query Routing: Directing requests to the correct shard efficiently.
Cross-Shard Joins: Minimizing joins between different shards.

Principles.Title: Indexing & Search
Principles.Effect: Optimizing data retrieval through specialized data structures and full-text search.
BestPractices: B-Tree vs Hash: Selecting indexing types for range vs point queries.
Inverted Indexing: Enabling efficient full-text searching.
Index Maintenance: Monitoring write performance impact.
Distributed Search: Scaling search across multiple clusters.

Description: Securing data flow and optimizing global content delivery.

Principles.Title: Load Balancing
Principles.Effect: Distributing incoming network traffic across a group of backend servers.
BestPractices: Health Checks: Dynamically removing failed nodes from the pool.
Sticky Sessions: Ensuring session persistence for stateful apps.
SSL Offloading: Decrypting HTTPS at the balancer level.
Global Server Load Balancing (GSLB): Routing based on location.

Principles.Title: Content Delivery (CDN)
Principles.Effect: A system of distributed servers that deliver web content based on the geographic locations of the user.
BestPractices: Edge Caching: Storing static assets close to end-users.
Dynamic Acceleration: Optimizing routes between edge and origin.
Security at Edge: Implementing WAF and DDoS protection.
Brotli/Gzip: Enabling advanced compression.

Principles.Title: API Design & Security
Principles.Effect: Building robust and secure interfaces for interoperability between systems.
BestPractices: OAuth2 / OIDC: Implementing industry-standard authentication.
Rate Limiting: Protecting endpoints from abuse and spikes.
Idempotency Keys: Preventing duplicate processing of requests.
API Versioning: managing lifecycle without breaking clients.

Principles.Title: Observability
Principles.Effect: Measuring the internal state of a system by examining its external outputs (logs, metrics, traces).
BestPractices: Distributed Tracing: Tracking requests across microservices.
Structured Logging: Emitting logs in machine-readable formats.
Anomaly Detection: Setting up automated alerts for unusual patterns.
Dashboards: Visualizing real-time system health.
